title: The Architecture of Michelangelo's Laurentian Library
highlights: Library The Laurentian Library, part of the complex of the Basilica of San Lorenzo, was part of Pope Clement VII’s plan to  ...    house Lorenzo de’ Medici’s collection of books, and what better artist to conceive it than Michelangelo? The great Michelangelo’s approach to the Laurentian Library is absolutely revolutionary, incorporating clever practical solutions  ...    Below these double columns are giant volutes (scrolls) that seem to hang there like tongues – they, too, serve no purpose. And in fact they are rather too large for the space, so that in the corners they, as James Ackerman says, “seem to mate rather than meet”. The stairway itself is also particularly impractical and unusual. I have always felt that it looks like the flow of lava spilling down onto the terracotta floor.
